audioText: Good morning. The museum will open at 10 a.m. today instead of 9 a.m. due to maintenance work. We apologize for any inconvenience. The café will open as usual at 9:30. Thank you for your understanding.

Attention passengers on flight 237 to Denver. The flight is delayed by 90 minutes due to severe weather. Please proceed to gate B12 for further updates. We will provide meal vouchers at the gate. Thank you for your patience.

audioText: You have reached the office of Dr. Sarah Chen. Our regular hours are Monday through Friday, 9 AM to 5 PM. If you have a medical emergency, please hang up and call 911. For appointments, press 1. For prescription refills, press 2. For billing, press 3. Thank you.

audioText: And now for today's weather. Sunny in the morning, but clouds will move in by noon. High of 72 degrees. There's a 60% chance of rain this evening, so keep an umbrella handy. Winds will be light from the south at 5 to 10 miles per hour.

audioText: Welcome to the Riverdale Factory Tour. My name is Tom, and I'll be your guide today. Please stay with the group at all times. Safety glasses are required in the production area. The tour will last about 45 minutes. We'll begin in the packaging section. No photography is allowed. Thank you.

audioText: And now for local traffic. A crash on the I-95 northbound near exit 42 has blocked two lanes. Expect delays of at least 30 minutes. The southbound side is moving normally. Alternate route: take Route 1 to avoid the backup. This traffic update is brought to you by City Tires.

Thank you for calling City Power. Your current estimated wait time is 8 minutes. Please have your account number ready. For payment options, press 1. To report an outage, press 2. For new service, press 3. You can also visit our website at citypower.com. Your call is important to us.

audioText: Hello everyone, and welcome to the annual staff meeting. I'm your HR director, Maria. First, I'd like to thank you all for your hard work this quarter. We'll start with the safety update, followed by the new vacation policy. Please turn off your phones. The meeting should end by 11:30.

audioText: Hi Mom, it's me. Just letting you know I landed safely. The flight was smooth. I'll be at the hotel in about an hour. I'll call you tomorrow morning. Love you, bye.

audioText: Are you tired of high electricity bills? Switch to SolarWave today. For a limited time, we're offering free installation for the first 100 customers. Save up to 40% on your monthly bill. Call 1-800-SOLAR now for a free quote. Don't wait – offer ends Friday.

Good morning, students. This is a reminder that the final exam for Math 101 will be held in room 203 on Friday at 9 AM. You must bring a calculator and a photo ID. No cell phones are allowed. The exam is two hours long. Please arrive 10 minutes early.

Attention: The 5:30 ferry to Oak Island has been canceled due to engine trouble. The next ferry will depart at 7:00 PM. Passengers with reservations will be automatically moved to the next sailing. Refunds are available at the ticket office. We apologize for the disruption.

audioText: Now presenting the new X-Phone 12. It has a 48-megapixel camera and battery life of up to 48 hours. The screen is 20% brighter than the previous model. Pre-orders start next Monday. The price is $799. Get yours at any authorized retailer.

audioText: Good evening. This is the principal speaking. Due to the snowstorm, all schools in the district will be closed tomorrow. Please check our website for updates on after-school activities. Stay warm and safe. Classes will resume on Monday unless otherwise announced.

Welcome to the City Art Museum. The museum is open from 10 AM to 6 PM daily. Admission is $15 for adults, $10 for seniors, and free for children under 12. Today's special exhibition on modern sculpture is on the third floor. Audio guides are available at the front desk for $5.

audioText: Welcome to the Grand Canyon helicopter tour. My name is Jake, and I'll be your pilot. Please keep your seatbelts fastened at all times. Headsets are provided for narration. The tour lasts 25 minutes. We'll fly over the South Rim first, then the North Rim. No smoking on board.

This is a public service announcement. Due to the heatwave, a cooling center has been opened at the community center on 5th Street. It is open from 12 PM to 8 PM today and tomorrow. Water and snacks are available. Please check on elderly neighbors. Stay hydrated.

audioText: And now for a look at the weekend forecast. Saturday will be partly cloudy with a high of 65. Sunday, expect rain in the morning, clearing by the afternoon. High near 60. Winds will be light. A good weekend to stay indoors on Sunday morning. Back to you, Tom.

audioText: Welcome to the Johnson & Sons annual picnic. The barbecue will start at 12:30 PM. There are games for children near the playground. Please remember to clean up your trash. The raffle drawing will be at 2 PM. Prizes include gift cards and a weekend getaway. Enjoy the day!

Attention, shoppers. We have a lost child at customer service. A 4-year-old girl in a pink dress. If you are looking for your child, please come to the customer service desk at the front of the store. Thank you for your help.

This is a reminder that the office will be closed on Monday for the national holiday. We will reopen on Tuesday at 9 AM. Please plan accordingly. For urgent matters, you can reach the on-call manager at 555-0199. Have a safe holiday weekend.

Your attention, please. The pool will close in 15 minutes for cleaning. All swimmers must exit the water by 7:45 PM. The locker rooms will remain open until 8:30 PM. Thank you for swimming at the Community Center. We hope to see you again tomorrow.
